 {"id":"bd-ta3b","title":"C.1: Implement gshadow line parser in frankenlibc-core","description":"## Goal
Add a gshadow line parser to frankenlibc-core, following the exact pattern of the existing /etc/passwd parser in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

## Reference: Existing Pattern
The passwd parser at pwd/mod.rs uses:
- `pub struct Passwd { pw_name: Vec<u8>, pw_passwd: Vec<u8>, ... }`
- `pub fn parse_passwd_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — splits on colons, validates field count
- `pub fn lookup_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — iterates lines, matches name

The gshadow parser should follow this EXACT pattern for consistency.

## gshadow File Format (/etc/gshadow)
Each line: `group_name:encrypted_password:administrators:members`
- group_name: non-empty, no colons (same as /etc/group name field)
- encrypted_password: may be empty (""), or "!" (locked), or "*" (no password)
- administrators: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no admins)
- members: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no members)

Example lines:
  root:*::
  shadow:!::
  sudo:!::alice,bob
  dev:x:alice:alice,bob,charlie

## Implementation

### File: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs (NEW FILE)
Add `pub mod gshadow;` to crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

### Struct
```rust
pub struct Gshadow {
    pub sg_namp: Vec<u8>,    // group name
    pub sg_passwd: Vec<u8>,  // encrypted password
    pub sg_adm: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated admin list
    pub sg_mem: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated member list
}
```

### Functions
```rust
pub fn parse_gshadow_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
pub fn lookup_gshadow_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
```

### Parsing Logic
1. Skip empty lines and lines starting with #
2. Split on b':' — must have exactly 4 fields
3. Field 0 (name) must be non-empty
4. Fields 1-3 may be empty
5. Return Gshadow with owned Vec<u8> copies

## Inline Tests (#[cfg(test)])
- parse_valid_full_line: `sudo:!:admin1,admin2:user1,user2` -> all 4 fields correct
- parse_minimal_line: `root:*::` -> password="*", admins="", members=""
- parse_empty_password: `grp:::` -> password is empty
- parse_locked_password: `grp:!::` -> password is "!"
- parse_with_admins_and_members: `dev:x:alice:alice,bob` -> admins="alice", members="alice,bob"
- reject_too_few_fields: `root:*:` (3 colons needed, only 2) -> None
- reject_empty_name: `:*::` -> None
- reject_comment_line: `# comment` -> None
- reject_empty_line: "" -> None
- lookup_finds_entry: content with 3 lines, lookup "sudo" finds correct one
- lookup_returns_none_for_missing: lookup "nonexistent" returns None
- lookup_is_case_sensitive: "ROOT" does not match "root"

## Acceptance Criteria
Parser handles all gshadow field combinations. 12 inline tests pass. cargo clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:37.066192744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.720091402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.719809839Z","close_reason":"Implemented gshadow parser in frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs. Struct Gshadow with sg_namp/sg_passwd/sg_adm/sg_mem fields. parse_gshadow_line() handles comments, blank lines, field validation. lookup_gshadow_by_name() scans content for case-sensitive name match. 14 inline tests pass covering: valid full/minimal lines, empty/locked password, admin+member lists, reject malformed, lookup hit/miss/case sensitivity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pwd","stub-elimination"]}
 {"id":"bd-tbhb","title":"scan_wordexp_syntax misses bad characters inside parameter expansion","description":"`scan_wordexp_syntax` in `unistd_abi.rs` skips all character validation when `parameter_brace_depth > 0`. This means unquoted bad characters (like `|`, `&`, `;`) inside a brace expansion (e.g. `${foo|bar}`) will go undetected, and `wordexp` will return `0` instead of `WRDE_BADCHAR`, breaking parity with glibc.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:07.847147776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:00:20.086037646Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:59:50.078157645Z","close_reason":"Fixed missing bad character detection inside brace expansion in scan_wordexp_syntax.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-thm2","title":"Fix c11threads managed-attr/host-pthread init mismatch","description":"c11threads_abi::mtx_init was building a FrankenLibC-managed pthread mutexattr word and passing it into host pthread_mutex_init, which triggered a glibc __pthread_tpp_change_priority assertion in c11threads_abi_test. Fix the C11 mutex/condvar init lane so C11 synchronization objects are initialized consistently on the native-managed path, then verify the focused C11 suite and workspace ABI checks via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFrog","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:34:41.394889788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204943932Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204703667Z","close_reason":"Resolved C11 mutex/condvar init mismatch by keeping C11 synchronization initialization on the native-managed lane; focused c11threads tests now pass and workspace tests advance to unrelated dlfcn failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1358,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented scoped native-mutex delegation helpers in pthread_abi and switched c11threads mutex/condvar init to use the native-managed lane. Verification: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test test_mtx_init_lock_unlock_destroy -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed; `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed (36/36); `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed; `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` completed without diffs. Workspace-wide `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` is still running under rch session 98302.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:36:15Z"},{"id":1359,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Broader verification update: `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets` no longer aborts in c11threads; the next remaining workspace failure is unrelated in `-p frankenlibc-abi --test dlfcn_abi_test` (14 failures, first at `dlerror_consumed_after_read`, then lock poisoning across dlopen/dlsym/dlvsym tests). This confirms the c11threads glibc assertion is resolved.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54Z"}]}
